*-------------.   Visit each of the following eight Wikipedia pages and extract the two specific facts listed for each topic.  Present your findings as a structured fact matrix (table) with one row per topic and columns for each fact.  Cite every Wikipedia URL.

1. Great Wall of China: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China — Extract: (a) total length in km or miles AND (b) the dynasty that built the most famous sections
2. CRISPR gene editing: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing — Extract: (a) the protein used as molecular scissors AND (b) the year CRISPR was first used to edit mammalian cells
3. Mariana Trench: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench — Extract: (a) maximum depth in metres or feet AND (b) name of the deepest point
4. Voyager 1: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1 — Extract: (a) launch year AND (b) the boundary it crossed to enter interstellar space
5. Rosetta Stone: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone — Extract: (a) the three scripts on the stone AND (b) the year it was discovered
6. Penicillin: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin — Extract: (a) who discovered penicillin AND (b) the year it was discovered
7. Aurora (astronomy): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy) — Extract: (a) the scientific name for the northern lights AND (b) what causes auroras (solar particles interacting with …)
8. Enigma machine: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enigma_machine — Extract: (a) who led the British effort to crack Enigma AND (b) the war in which Enigma was used

After gathering all facts, present a MARKDOWN TABLE with columns: Topic | Fact A | Fact B | Source URL

1a715346-9668-413e-9e8e-6d8f5b811b57.result_content_preview:
The
Enigma machine
is a
cipher
device developed and used in the early- to mid-20th century to protect
commercial
, diplomatic, and military communication. It was employed extensively by
Nazi Germany
during
World War II
, in all branches of the
German military
. The Enigma machine was considered so secure that it was used to encipher the most top-secret messages.

rotor mechanism

final_output.final_deliverable:
Below I present the requested fact matrix (one row per topic). For each extracted fact I (a) cite the Wikipedia URL used, and (b) provide a verbatim quote from the RAW VISIT CONTENT showing the fact was taken from that page. If a requested fact could not be extracted from the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT, I state exactly what is missing.

Detailed findings (verbatim evidence + citation) — one topic per section:

1) Great Wall of China
- Fact A — Total length:
  - Verbatim quote(s) from the page: "Size 21,196.18 km (13,170.70 mi)" and "spanning 21,196.18 km (13,170.70 mi) in total."
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China

- Fact B — Dynasty that built the most famous sections:
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "the best-known sections were built by the Ming dynasty (1368–1644)."
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China

2) CRISPR gene editing
- Fact A — Protein used as molecular "scissors":
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "By delivering the Cas9 nuclease complexed with a synthetic guide RNA (gRNA) into a cell, the cell's genome can be cut at a desired location..."
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing

- Fact B — The year CRISPR was first used to edit mammalian cells (as stated on this page):
  - Finding from RAW VISIT CONTENT: The provided RAW VISIT CONTENT for the CRISPR page includes clear statements that Cas9 is used to cut genomes and discusses the discovery/publication timeline (e.g., identification of CRISPR–Cas9 in 2012 and patent and commercialization milestones), but the page text in the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT does NOT contain an explicit, verbatim statement of "the year CRISPR was first used to edit mammalian cells." The page does include statements showing CRISPR–Cas9 enabled editing in eukaryotic cells, for example: "Cas9, derived from the bacterial species Streptococcus pyogenes, has facilitated targeted genomic modification in eukaryotic cells by enabling a reliable method of creating a targeted break..." but it does not state a single explicit year for "first used to edit mammalian cells" in the RAW content supplied here.
  - Verbatim quote(s) demonstrating what is present (but not the explicit year): "Cas9, derived from the bacterial species Streptococcus pyogenes, has facilitated targeted genomic modification in eukaryotic cells by enabling a reliable method of creating a targeted break at a specific location as designated by the crRNA and tracrRNA guide strands." 
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing
  - Conclusion: The page (in the RAW VISIT CONTENT provided) does not contain an explicit single-line statement giving "the year CRISPR was first used to edit mammalian cells." Therefore I could not extract that specific year from the RAW VISIT CONTENT supplied. If you want, I can (a) fetch the page again and search the live page for an explicit year statement or (b) provide the widely-cited year from the primary literature (with citation) outside the RAW content you provided.

3) Mariana Trench
- Fact A — Maximum depth:
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "Max. depth 10,984 ± 25 metres (36,037 ± 82 ft; 6,006 ± 14 fathoms; 6.825 ± 0.016 mi) (Challenger Deep)" and "The maximum known depth is 10,984 ± 25 metres (36,037 ± 82 ft; 6,006 ± 14 fathoms; 6.825 ± 0.016 mi) at the southern end of a small slot-shaped valley in its floor known as the Challenger Deep."
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench

- Fact B — Name of the deepest point:
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "...known as the Challenger Deep." (from the sentence above) and elsewhere the page references "the deepest part of the trench... the Challenger Deep."
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench

4) Voyager 1
- Fact A — Launch year:
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "Voyager 1 is a space probe launched by NASA on September 5, 1977, as part of the Voyager program..." (shows launch date and year 1977)
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1

- Fact B — Boundary it crossed to enter interstellar space:
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause and entered interstellar space on August 25, 2012, making it the first spacecraft to do so." (identifies the boundary as the "heliopause")
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1

5) Rosetta Stone
- Fact A — The three scripts on the stone:
  - Verbatim quote from the page (infobox and description): "Writing Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs, Demotic script, and Greek script" and "It bears three inscriptions: the top register in Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs, the second in the Egyptian Demotic script, and the third in Ancient Greek."
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone

- Fact B — Year it was discovered:
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "Discovered 1799 near Rosetta, Nile Delta, Egypt" and the rediscovery section: "It was found there in July 1799 by French army officer Pierre-François Bouchard during France's invasion of Egypt."
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone

6) Penicillin
- Requested extracts: (a) who discovered penicillin and (b) the year it was discovered.
- Issue / what is missing: The visit to the Penicillin Wikipedia page failed in the provided execution data. The MERGED/EXECUTION TRAIL shows the visit node for https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin returned an error: "HTTP fetch failed: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin status=None". Because the RAW VISIT CONTENT for the Penicillin page is NOT present in the materials you gave me, I cannot supply verbatim quotes from the Penicillin Wikipedia page here.
  - Evidence of the failure (from the execution trail / merge data): the visit action result: {"action": "visit", "success": false, "error": "HTTP fetch failed: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin status=None"} and the node shows the same URL.
  - Source URL (attempted): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin
- Conclusion: I was unable to extract the requested facts from the Penicillin page because the page fetch failed in the provided run. If you would like, I can re-fetch that specific page and extract the facts with verbatim quotes from the RAW content.

7) Aurora (astronomy)
- Fact A — Scientific name for the northern lights:
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "The terms northern lights (aurora borealis) and southern lights (aurora australis) are used in the Northern and Southern Hemispheres respectively." This identifies the scientific/Latinized term as "aurora borealis" for the northern lights.
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy)

- Fact B — What causes auroras (solar particles interacting with ...):
  - Verbatim quote from the page: "Auroras are the result of disturbances in the Earth's magnetosphere caused by enhanced speeds of solar wind from coronal holes and coronal mass ejections. These disturbances alter the trajectories of charged particles in the magnetospheric plasma. These particles, mainly electrons and protons, precipitate into the upper atmosphere (thermosphere/exosphere). The resulting ionization and excitation of atmospheric constituents emit light of varying colour and complexity." (this directly states the cause: solar-wind-related charged particles interacting with Earth's magnetosphere and upper atmosphere)
  - Source (URL): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy)

8) Enigma machine
- Fact A — Who led the British effort to crack Enigma:
  - Finding from RAW VISIT CONTENT provided: The Enigma machine page in the RAW VISIT CONTENT (that was supplied) clearly states that the Enigma was used extensively by Nazi Germany during World War II. However, in the portion of the RAW VISIT CONTENT included in the run, I could not locate a clear, verbatim line naming the single person who "led the British effort to crack Enigma" (such as a sentence explicitly naming "Alan Turing led the British effort...").
